blog_articles.title: The Ultimate Wedding Day Timeline Guide
blog_articles.excerpt: A step-by-step wedding day timeline from getting ready through the grand exit, with buffer time and photographer tips.
blog_articles.content: Planning a realistic wedding day timeline is one of the best ways to reduce stress and ensure beautiful, unhurried photos. In this guide we walk through a sample schedule from hair and makeup to the grand exit, with suggested times for first look, family formals, portraits, ceremony, and reception events. We also include buffer time recommendations and pro tips for working around sunset and travel between locations.

blog_articles.title: Sample 8-Hour Wedding Photography Timeline
blog_articles.excerpt: Use this sample 8-hour timeline to map out coverage from details to dance floor.
blog_articles.content: An 8-hour wedding photography package is one of the most popular options for couples. This sample timeline assumes a 4:00 PM ceremony and 10:00 PM reception end time. We break down where your photographer will be and what they will be capturing during each hour, including flat-lay details, getting ready, first look, ceremony, portraits, and reception highlights.

blog_articles.title: Designing a Beach Wedding Timeline Around Sunset
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn how to build a beach wedding timeline that maximizes golden hour light for portraits and ceremony.
blog_articles.content: Beach weddings are all about the light. In this article we show you how to reverse-engineer your wedding day schedule from sunset time, building in a relaxed pre-ceremony portrait session and plenty of cushion for tide changes, wind, and transportation. We include a sample day-of schedule and tips for coordinating with your planner and photographer.

blog_articles.title: Rainy Day Wedding Photography Tips
blog_articles.excerpt: Embrace the rain with clear umbrellas, backup locations, and creative lighting.
blog_articles.content: Weather is one thing you can’t control on your wedding day, but you can absolutely prepare for it. We share tips on choosing covered portrait locations, working with clear umbrellas, and building a flexible photo plan so your images still feel bright and joyful.

blog_articles.title: First Look vs. Aisle Reveal: Pros and Cons
blog_articles.excerpt: Should you see each other before the ceremony? We break down how each option affects your timeline and photos.
blog_articles.content: Choosing between a first look and waiting for the aisle reveal changes your photo timeline significantly. This article explores emotional considerations, portrait scheduling, and how to make the most of whichever option you choose.

blog_articles.title: 15 Must-Have Ceremony Photos
blog_articles.excerpt: From walking down the aisle to your first kiss, here are the ceremony moments you won’t want to miss.
blog_articles.content: We outline the essential ceremony photos most couples love, plus a few creative ideas that make your gallery feel uniquely yours.

blog_articles.title: Stress-Free Family Formals Checklist
blog_articles.excerpt: Organize your family photos so they’re quick, smooth, and drama-free.
blog_articles.content: Family formals don’t have to take an hour. Use our printable checklist and grouping tips to keep this part of your day organized and efficient.

blog_articles.title: How to Plan Photography for a Corporate Event
blog_articles.excerpt: Coverage recommendations and shot ideas for conferences, galas, and sales meetings.
blog_articles.content: From keynote sessions to cocktail hour networking, we break down how many hours of coverage you may need and which add-ons, like on-site printing, make sense for corporate events.

blog_articles.title: How to Prepare for Your Engagement Session
blog_articles.excerpt: Outfit tips, location ideas, and what to expect at your session.
blog_articles.content: Your engagement session is a low-pressure way to get comfortable in front of the camera. We cover what to wear, how to choose locations, and how to time your session for the best light.

blog_articles.title: How to Choose the Right Wedding Photographer
blog_articles.excerpt: Questions to ask, portfolio red flags, and how to compare packages.
blog_articles.content: We share a practical framework for reviewing portfolios, reading reviews, and understanding package options so you can confidently choose your photographer.

blog_articles.title: Printing Your Photos: Albums, Wall Art, and More
blog_articles.excerpt: Turn your favorite images into heirloom pieces instead of leaving them on a hard drive.
blog_articles.content: We outline the pros and cons of albums, framed prints, and canvases, plus how to choose the right sizes for your home.

blog_articles.title: Why Having Two Wedding Photographers Matters
blog_articles.excerpt: From multiple angles to better timeline coverage, here’s when a second photographer is worth it.
blog_articles.content: A second photographer allows your team to capture both partners getting ready, multiple angles during the ceremony, and more candid moments during cocktail hour. We explain how this impacts your wedding day timeline and which packages include a second photographer by default.

service_packages.description: Full-day coverage in June with one lead photographer, perfect for smaller weddings.

service_packages.description: Four hours of evening coverage for galas, fundraisers, and award ceremonies.

photographers.bio: Lena is a lead wedding photographer known for emotional storytelling and clean, timeless editing. She specializes in complex wedding day timelines, multi-cultural ceremonies, and working seamlessly with planners.
Emily blends documentary coverage with gentle posing to create galleries that feel candid yet polished. She is a favorite among couples who value detailed planning and relaxed portraits.
Daniel is an efficient, timeline-focused photographer who excels at organizing family formals and capturing clean, editorial-style images of your day.
Jordan is a calm, detail-oriented wedding photographer who loves photographing candid in-between moments and beautifully lit ceremonies.
Mia leads our corporate and event team, specializing in conferences, galas, and branding sessions with clean, consistent coverage that marketing teams love.
Avery is a corporate and portrait photographer focused on clear, brand-aligned imagery for businesses and professionals.
Sam photographs families and smaller weddings with a fun, relaxed approach that keeps kids engaged and adults comfortable.
Riley is a hybrid photographer who enjoys capturing both weddings and corporate events, with an emphasis on dynamic reception and dance floor images.
Isabella focuses on portrait and family work, creating soft, natural images that feel like your best self on your best day.
